Since the lake starts with the maximum population of 100 tons, if all fishermen catch a large amount, the fish population will decrease drastically, reducing future income for everyone. To maintain a sustainable fish population and ensure long-term income, it is best to fish moderately.

If I catch too much, the fish population will drop, and future catches will be smaller. If I catch too little, I might miss out on immediate income. A balanced approach is to catch a moderate amount, encouraging others to do the same.

Assuming others will also fish moderately, I will catch 10 tons this month to help maintain the fish population while earning a reasonable income.

Answer: 10

Since the lake starts with 100 tons of fish, if all fishermen catch a large amount, the fish population will decrease significantly, reducing future income. To maximize long-term income, it's best to fish sustainably.

If I catch too much this month, the fish population will drop, and future catches will be lower. If I catch too little, I might miss out on income this month.

Assuming others fish moderately, I will choose a moderate amount to balance immediate income and sustainability.

A reasonable choice is to catch 10 tons this month, hoping others do the same, so the total catch is 50 tons, leaving 50 tons in the lake. After reproduction, the fish population will be 100 tons again, maintaining sustainability.
